DECLARATION OF RENEE DE GOLIER IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF, UNITEDLAYER, LLCS MOTION FOR ORDER RE: RELIEF PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 2033.300 2033.280, 473(B) AND S 1008 [RE: JANUARY 18, 2024 ORDER DEEMING MATTERS ADMITTED ORDER RE OBJECTIONS] (TRANSACTION ID # 72070223) FILED BY PLAINTIFF UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF UNITEDLAYER, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER RE: RELIEF FROM WAIVER OF OBJECTIONS RE: MULTIPLE DISCOVERY SETS SERVED BY DEFENDANT, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO IS TO MARCH 12, 2024 AT 9:00 A.M. DEPT. 302 DISCOVERY FOR REHEARING BEFORE JPT MATTHEW KAHN. ANY ORDER GRANTING THIS MOTION WOULD CREATE AN INCONSISTENCY WITH THE ORDER OF JANUARY 18, 2024. UNDER CCP 1008, A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION MUST BE HEARD BY THE "SAME JUDGE." WHILE THIS IS NOT STYLED AS A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION, IT WOULD HAVE THAT EFFECT. THEREFORE, THE BETTER APPROACH IS TO CONTINUE THE PRESENT MOTION FOR RELIEF TO THE SAME JUDICIAL OFFICER WHO COULD RECONSIDER THE ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES. JUDGE PRO TEM MATTHEW KAHN'S NEXT DATE TO PRESIDE IN DISCOVERY IS MARCH 12, 2024. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

Motion For Order Re: Relief Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 2033.300 2433.284, S 473(B) And S 1008 [Re: January 8,2024 Order Deeming Matters Admitted And Order Re: Objections]
Off Calendar Mar-12-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Renee De Golier, Esq. CHRISTENSEN HSU SIPES LLP (310) 222-8607 ed@chs.law | renee@chs.law
Attorney for Plaintiff, UNITEDLAYER, LLC
DAVID R. HOBSTETTER, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3981 david.hobstetter@sfcityatty.org Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
Shiela Pham, CSR#13293, 415-274-9977, shielapham28@gmail.com Court reporter,

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF UNITEDLAYER, LLC,'S Motion For Order Re: Relief Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 2033.300 2433.284, S 473(B) And S 1008 [Re: January 8,2024 Order Deeming Matters Admitted And Order Re: Objections]

The motion is DENIED. The motion appears to be untimely because it was not made within 10 days of the prior order, and even if it were not untimely, Plaintiff has not identified any viable basis for reconsideration. Plaintiff has neither identified new facts that could not have been presented to the Court before the prior order nor demonstrated excusable neglect.

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn at mkahn@gibsondunn.com. Counsel is instructed by the Court to submit a single proposed order encompassing both motions heard today that clearly indentifies each motion ruling.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Shiela Pham, CSR#13293, 415-274-9977, shielapham28@gmail.com Court reporter, Reported. (302/RBU)

DAVID R. HOBSTETTER, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3981 david.hobstetter@sfcityatty.org Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
Shiela Pham, CSR#13293, 415-274-9977, shielapham28@gmail.com Court reporter,

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF UNITEDLAYER, LLC,'S Motion For Order Re: Relief From Waiver Of Objections Re: Multiple Discovery Sets Served By Defendant, City And County Of San Francisco

The motion is DENIED. Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the prior failure to timely respond and object to discovery was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Plaintiff also had not, at the time the motion was filed, served Code-compliant responses and objections (other than, potentially, to the Requests for Admission, which already have been deemed admitted without objection pursuant to a separate order of the Court).

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn at mkahn@gibsondunn.com. Counsel is instructed by the Court to submit a single proposed order encompassing both motions heard today that clearly indentifies each motion ruling.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Shiela Pham, CSR#13293, 415-274-9977, shielapham28@gmail.com Court reporter, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion For Order Re: Relief From Waiver Of Objections Re: Multiple Discovery Sets Served By Defendant, City And County Of San Francisco
Off Calendar Feb-20-2024 Continued To Mar-12-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as modified:

PLAINTIFF UNITEDLAYER, LLC'S Motion For Order Re: Relief From Waiver Of Objections Re: Multiple Discovery Sets Served By Defendant, City And County Of San Francisco

Motion CONTINUED to March 12, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. Dept. 302 Disocvery. for rehearing before JPT Matthew Kahn. Any order granting this motion would create an inconsistency with the order of January 18, 2024. Under CCP 1008, a motion for reconsideration must be heard by the "same judge." While this is not styled as a motion for reconsideration, it would have that effect. Therefore, the better approach is to continue the present motion for relief to the same judicial officer who could reconsider the order compelling responses. Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn's next date to preside in Discovery is March 12, 2024.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

DEFENDANT CIT AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL AND TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED (ADDED BACK TO CALENDAR PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES AND DUE TO A CLERICAL ERROR; EMAIL DATED 11/17/23)
Off Calendar Jan-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT CIT AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL AND TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Renee De Golier, Esq., of CHRISTENSEN HSU SIPES LLP, (310) 222-8607, for Plaintiff.

No other appearances.

At the outset, judge pro tem Matthew Kahn reminds counsel that there was no indication of an intent to appear to contest the court's tentative ruling. The matter is discussed.

Upon conclusion of discussion, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall serve written responses, without objections, to the City's Form Interrogatories (set one), Special Interrogatories (set one), and Requests for Production of Documents (set one), within 7 days of this Order. The genuineness of any documents and the truth of any matters specified in the City's Requests for Admissions (set one) are deemed admitted.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at MKahn@gibsondunn.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
